---
title: "Week 13 — Categorical outcomes"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

How do we compare rates and proportions?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Risk difference, relative risk, and odds ratio; two-way tables and
the basic chi-square logic.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Compute and interpret a risk difference and relative risk from a
clinical two-way table.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new clinical or biological
categorical-outcome case.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 16 (one-prop), Ch 17 (two-props), and Ch 18 (tables).

## Supplemental source / custom needs

ISLBS clinical RR/OR examples once mirrored; custom risk/odds
clusters drawn from health and life-science contexts.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Compute and interpret basic risks.
- [ ] Interpret risk difference and relative risk.
- [ ] Recognize odds language.
- [ ] Read two-way tables and basic chi-square output.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
